headline: Figma MCP Goes Multi-IDE
subheadline: Figma just expanded its MCP server to support two-way workflows with Cursor and more coding tools — directly relevant to the Figma MCP + Claude integration you're building. Plus: AI design tokens hit GA, and a fresh conference just wrapped on AI-powered design systems.

sections.label: Trending
sections.title: Figma MCP now supports two-way workflows with Cursor, Warp, and more
sections.description: This is big for you specifically. Figma's MCP server now enables push-and-pull workflows across Cursor, Warp, Factory, Firebender, and Augment — not just one-way reads. You can push rendered UI to the canvas as editable frames, compare flows with your team, then pull design context back into code. Given you're already building a Figma MCP + Claude integration for white-label sport apps, this expanded ecosystem could change your architecture choices.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Figma's 2026 updates quietly redefine design-dev handoff
sections.description: AI Design Tokens just hit General Availability — Figma's AI now suggests color, spacing, and typography tokens based on existing codebases and writes them out. For your white-label design system work, this could massively accelerate token generation across sport app variants instead of manual mapping. Worth a quick scan to see how it handles codebase-informed suggestions.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: Cursor rolls out a new agentic coding system
sections.description: Cursor 2.0 shipped Background Agents, Hooks, and a proper Plan Mode that makes AI coding feel less like vibes and more like a structured workflow. Since you're vibe-coding with Cursor daily, the Background Agents feature — where tasks keep running while you context-switch — could genuinely change how productive your sessions are, especially on limited time during maternity leave.

sections.label: Concept of the day
sections.title: Agentic design systems: AI orchestrating the design-to-code pipeline
sections.description: 2026's shift isn't AI assisting designers — it's AI agents orchestrating the full design-to-code pipeline autonomously. High-performing teams are using MCP to let agents read structured design data, generate code, and validate consistency without human handoff at every step. This is the trajectory your Figma MCP + Claude project is already on — this piece frames the bigger strategic picture well.

sections.label: Watch
sections.title: Into Design Systems: AI Design Systems Conference 2026 (just wrapped)
sections.description: This two-day online conference on AI-powered design systems happened March 19-20 — literally yesterday. 1,000+ design system professionals attended. Recordings should be going up soon. Given your deep expertise in design systems and your current MCP project, the sessions on AI-driven token management and agentic workflows could be goldmine content for stroller walks if they release audio.

sections.label: Read
sections.title: AI in 2026: Five product shifts redefining digital UX
sections.description: A thoughtful piece on what's actually slowing AI down in product UX today — and five emerging design patterns that address it. As someone evolving toward product builder / product leadership, this frames the strategic design decisions you'll be making in your next role. Less tactical, more "how should we think about designing AI-native products."

sections.label: Think about this
sections.title: If AI agents can orchestrate design-to-code, what's the senior designer's new superpower?
sections.description: With Figma MCP enabling two-way flows, AI tokens auto-generating from codebases, and agentic tools running background tasks — the manual craft layer is thinning fast. Your deep systems thinking and white-label architecture expertise become rarer, not less valuable. But the role description changes. Worth sitting with: when you return, do you want to lead a design team or lead a product that happens to need design?
